_description: REFERENCE: page-block config for the global sys-scroll-sequence component. NOT a component-create body — sys-scroll-sequence is already published (is_global=true), you reference it by slug in a page's blocks[] array. Produce {base_url, pattern, count} via the SpiderVideo extract_frames job (see examples/scroll-sequence.sh).
_component_reference: sys-scroll-sequence v1.0.0 (Tier 3 — deps: gsap, gsap/ScrollTrigger). Shipped in the global component registry. Do NOT re-create it per client.
_usage: 1. Run examples/scroll-sequence.sh OR use the scroll-sequence recipe skill
2. Grab {base_url, pattern, count} from the extract_frames job manifest
3. POST the block below inside your page's blocks[] array

_props_schema.base_url: string — absolute URL prefix ending with / where all frames live
_props_schema.pattern: string — filename pattern with {index} or {index:04d} placeholder
_props_schema.scroll_distance_vh: integer (default 400) — how much viewport-height of scroll the sequence occupies
_props_schema.canvas_width: integer (default 1280) — source frame width for aspect preservation
_props_schema.canvas_height: integer (default 720) — source frame height for aspect preservation
_props_schema.preload_strategy: enum 'progressive' (default, ±15 frame window) or 'all' (full upfront preload)
_props_schema.background_color: string (default '#000000') — canvas fill before first frame paints

_anti_patterns: DON'T pass image_urls: [...] — sys-scroll-sequence v1.0.0 doesn't accept it. Use the pattern form.
DON'T set preload_strategy='all' for >60 frames — you'll DDoS the CDN and get throttled.
DON'T hardcode base_url with a pinggy/serveo tunnel — those proxies inject HTML interstitials that break frame decoding.
